TSLA: Broadstone adds Tesla recycling project as build-to-suit pipeline nears $180M. Broadstone Net Lease announced its Q1 2026 results, reporting a 5.6% year-over-year AFFO growth and adding $30 million to its build-to-suit pipeline, now totaling nearly $180 million. A significant new development includes a $30.4 million Tesla battery recycling facility in Austin, Texas, expected to stabilize in Q4 2027. The company's diversified portfolio consists of 773 net leased commercial properties and it reaffirmed its 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.53 to $1.57 per diluted share.

Matthews International Reports Q2 Results, Maintains 2026 Outlook. Matthews International (MATW) reported mixed Q2 2026 results, with growth in Memorialization and Product Identification offsetting declines in Industrial Technologies. The company maintained its full-year 2026 outlook, including adjusted EBITDA guidance of $180 million, bolstered by a favorable arbitration ruling with Tesla, progress in its Propelis joint venture, and strategic debt reduction and divestitures aimed at enhancing shareholder value.

Tesla Semi finally begins mass production. Tesla has officially started high-volume production of its Semi electric truck at Gigafactory Nevada. The company announced the milestone on X, indicating that the first Semi has rolled off its new production line. Deliveries of the electric truck are anticipated to commence later this year.

First Tesla Semi rolls off high volume production line. Tesla announced that its first Semi truck has rolled off a high-volume production line, indicating it is on schedule for large-scale manufacturing of several new products in 2026. The all-electric truck is designed for long-haul freight with a range of up to 500 miles on a single charge. Tesla also plans to begin volume production of its Cybercab robotaxi and Megapack 3 battery system in 2026, with significant capital spending focused on expanding factories for these initiatives.

Broadstone Net Lease Announces First Quarter 2026 Results and Adds $30 million to its Committed Pipeline of Build-to-Suit Developments. Broadstone Net Lease (BNL) announced its Q1 2026 operating results, reporting a 5.6% year-over-year AFFO growth and a net income of $46.4 million. The company invested $171.9 million in acquisitions and build-to-suit developments, including adding $30 million to its committed pipeline, exemplified by a new Tesla battery recycling facility project. BNL also reaffirmed its 2026 AFFO guidance of $1.53 to $1.57 per diluted share and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2925 per share.

TD Bank (NYSE: TD) issues auto-callable notes with ≥14.85% coupon. The Toronto-Dominion Bank is offering market-linked, auto-callable notes (Senior Debt Securities, Series H) with a fixed monthly coupon of at least 14.85% per annum. The notes are tied to the lowest performing common stock of AMD, META, MU, and TSLA, with a 20% buffered downside. While offering a high coupon, investors face the risk of losing up to 80% of the face amount if the lowest performing stock falls significantly below 80% of its starting price, and payments are subject to the Bank's credit risk.

Tesla sued after worker says he was struck by carbon steel pipe. A welder at Tesla's Gigafactory Texas, Juan Jesus Chavez, is suing Tesla Inc. and his employer, EFX Industrial, after being struck by a carbon steel pipe. The lawsuit claims negligence by both companies, citing unsafe working conditions, improper training, and inadequate safety equipment. Chavez is seeking over $1 million in damages for his injuries, which include a closed head injury and orthopedic issues.

Intel Stock Surges to All-Time High on Foundry Revival and Strong CPU Demand. Intel's stock has reached a new all-time high of $94.10 per share, marking over 400% growth in a single year, driven by the revival of its Foundry business and robust CPU demand. The company's success is attributed to government investment, improved yields across its Intel 4, Intel 3, and 18A nodes, and significant customer wins like Tesla. Additionally, Intel is repurposing silicon dies that would otherwise be scrap, demonstrating strong product execution and demand for its CPUs.

Is Free Tesla Supercharging for a Year a Good Deal?. Tesla is offering one free year of Supercharging with the purchase of a Model 3 Premium or Performance. The value of this offer varies significantly depending on location and charging habits, potentially saving drivers $450-$950. The article breaks down the calculation, considering average mileage and electricity rates, and notes that most savings will be realized by those who frequently use Superchargers over home charging.

Gigafactory Texas injury lawsuit: Welder hit by pipe sues Tesla, contractor. A welder at Tesla's Gigafactory Texas is suing Tesla and his employer, EFX Industrial, for negligence after he was struck by a carbon steel pipe in March. Juan Jesus Chavez claims insufficient safety protocols and lack of training led to him sustaining a closed head injury and orthopedic injuries, seeking over $1 million in damages. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about safety at the plant, especially after Travis County recently withheld some of Tesla's tax breaks due to incomplete documentation on construction site safety.
TGT: Target opens $265 million upstream warehouse to service 6 DCs. Target Corporation has opened its first "receive center," a 1.2-million square foot warehouse in Houston, representing a $265 million investment. This new facility will collect goods directly from global vendors and hold them until needed, replenishing inventory for six regional distribution centers and one flow center. The strategic location and operational model aim to improve product delivery speed, reduce costs, and prevent overcrowding in existing distribution centers and store backrooms.

Costco Is '60% Cheaper Than Traditional Grocery,' Says JPMorgan—And The Price War Is Getting One-Sided. JPMorgan reports that Costco Wholesale Corp. is widening its value gap, offering prices "up to 60% cheaper than traditional grocery" and about 30% cheaper than Walmart and Target. This significant price differential is attributed to Costco's unique business model and is compelling consumers to shift their shopping habits, putting immense pressure on traditional grocers and other big-box retailers. The article concludes that in a high-cost environment, Costco's low-cost operation allows it to further solidify its market position.

Target opens first-of-its-kind receive center in Houston area, creating 185 jobs. Target has opened a first-of-its-kind, 1.2 million-square-foot receive center in the Houston area, representing a $265 million investment. This new facility will supply inventory to seven distribution centers across the region and is expected to create 185 jobs.

Target opens 'first-of-its-kind;’ 1.2 million-sq.-ft. “receive center'. Target has opened its first-ever "receive center" in Houston, a 1.2 million-sq.-ft. facility designed to intake products directly from global vendors and hold them until needed. This $265 million hub aims to replenish inventory faster and at lower costs, complementing Target's existing import warehouses by adding regional capacity. The center, which is expected to create 185 new jobs, is part of Target's broader strategy to enhance its supply chain efficiency and better manage seasonal or hard-to-forecast items.

Target opens its first-ever 'receive center'. Target has launched its first "receive center" in Houston, a 1.2 million-sq.-ft. facility designed to intake products directly from global vendors and hold them until needed, replenishing inventory in response to demand. This $265 million hub aims to improve distribution efficiency, reduce costs, and prevent overcrowding by allowing Target to distribute inventory precisely when required. The center, which will service six regional distribution centers and one flow center, is expected to create 185 new jobs and is part of Target's ongoing efforts to enhance its supply chain and "stores as hubs" strategy.
SBUX: What's Behind The Drop In Starbucks Stock?. Starbucks (SBUX) stock is falling ahead of its earnings report, with investors focused on the company's long-term strategy in China and its ability to meet profitability and revenue targets. Analysts have a mixed view on the stock's valuation, and key metrics like same-store sales and digital engagement will be crucial to watch.

Niccol Effect Has Starbucks Stock Barreling Toward Fresh Highs. Starbucks' stock is surging towards new highs due to the "Niccol Effect," a term describing CEO Brian Niccol's successful strategy previously seen at Chipotle Mexican Grill. This strategy emphasizes a digital-first approach, leading to accelerated organic growth, improved unit economics, and enhanced profitability, as evidenced by Starbucks' strong Q2 2026 results. Analysts are lifting price targets, and institutional investors are accumulating shares, positioning SBUX for a potential major breakout.

Starbucks Brews Breakout: Sales Beat, Traffic Hits 3-Year High - Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX). Starbucks reported strong fiscal second-quarter results, beating sales and earnings estimates, with global same-store sales up 6.2% and North America sales growing 7.1%. The company saw its best traffic in three years, driven by marketing, menu innovation, and operational improvements. Analysts from BTIG, TD Cowen, Goldman Sachs, and DA Davidson reacted positively, with several raising price targets, as management also increased its fiscal 2026 earnings guidance.

SBUX (NASDAQ: SBUX) files Form 144; insider sold 2,500 shares. Starbucks (SBUX) filed a Form 144, indicating a proposed sale of Common Stock. The filing details prior issuances, including an ESPP purchase and restricted stock vesting. Additionally, it reveals that Sara-Lynn Kelly sold 2,500 shares on March 5, 2026, for $242,800.
DIS: Cannon Capital Management Inc. Invests $1.51 Million in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Cannon Capital Management Inc. has acquired a new stake of 13,295 shares in The Walt Disney Company, amounting to approximately $1.51 million, making it 1.3% of their holdings. Other major investors like Viking Global and Vanguard also adjusted their positions, with institutional investors now holding about 65.71% of Disney stock. The company faces mixed sentiment, with positive news regarding ESPN's future within Disney but negative sentiment around regulatory and political controversies impacting ABC licenses and DEI practices.

The Walt Disney Company & King Jouet, launch first-ever “Star Wars™ Store by King Jouet” pop-up store in Paris. The Walt Disney Company and King Jouet have launched the first-ever "Star Wars™ Store by King Jouet" pop-up store in Paris. This 300m² immersive experience, located in Les Halles, features two dedicated spaces for classic characters and "The Mandalorian and Grogu," designed to celebrate upcoming Star Wars releases and anniversaries. The store offers exclusive products, experiential events, a lightsaber creation station, a Star Wars-themed café, and over 1,000 product references including books, toys, fashion, and beauty items.

Cinematic Fruit Partnerships. Del Monte has announced a partnership with Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5 in the UK to bring animated elements to their fresh fruit ranges. This collaboration aims to promote fruit purchases, offering on-pack promotions and consumer competitions with exclusive prizes between May and September 2026. The initiative leverages cinematic magic to enhance grocery retail experiences and drive cross-media consumer engagement.

Cinematic Fruit Partnerships. Del Monte and Disney and Pixar have announced a partnership for the upcoming release of "Toy Story 5," bringing cinematic magic to the fruit aisle in the UK and other international markets from May to September 2026. This collaboration will feature animated elements on Del Monte's fresh, prepared, and freshly cut products, aiming to boost fruit purchases and offer exclusive prizes through an on-pack promotion and consumer competition. This initiative highlights trends in branded fresh-produce collaborations, movie tie-in retail promotions, and animated packaging experiences, with implications for grocery retail, consumer packaged goods, and entertainment marketing partnerships.
